title: cassia glauca var. suffruticosa
intro: Cassia glauca var. suffruticosa, also known as Senna auriculata, is a small to medium-sized shrub native to various parts of the world, including India and Africa. The plant's medicinal properties are primarily attributed to its seeds, which contain anthraquinone glycosides, a compound that stimulates bowel movements and has laxative effects. Cassia glauca var. suffruticosa is used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including constipation, diarrhea, and skin conditions, and has also been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in Ayurvedic medicine, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
